content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup:
- MongoDB replica set `rs0` with 3 pods: mongo-rs-0, mongo-rs-1, mongo-rs-2
- Running without TLS
- openssl-toolbox pod available for certificate generation
- Need to mount certs at /etc/tls/ with ca.crt, server.pem, client.pem

My plan:
1. Generate CA key and certificate using openssl-toolbox
2. Generate server key and CSR, sign with CA → server.pem (cert+key combined)
3. Do the same for client certificates
4. Store everything in a Kubernetes Secret
5. Update the StatefulSet to mount the secret and configure mongod with TLS settings, including the certificate paths and CA file
6. Test that unencrypted connections are rejected but TLS connections work

Let me check what's available in the openssl-toolbox pod first.

content.text: Now I have a clear picture. Let me generate the certificates using the openssl-toolbox pod, then update the StatefulSet to require TLS.

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- openssl version 2>/dev/null

kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- bash -c '
set -e

# Generate CA key and self-signed cert
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/ca.key 4096

openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key /tmp/ca.key \
  -sha256 -days 3650 \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-ca/O=karma" \
  -out /tmp/ca.crt

# Generate server key + CSR + cert
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/server.key 2048

cat > /tmp/server.cnf <<EOF
[req]
req_extensions = v3_req
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
[req_distinguished_name]
[v3_req]
subjectAltName = @alt_names
[alt_names]
DNS.1 = localhost
DNS.2 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.3 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.4 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb
DNS.6 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb
DNS.7 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb
DNS.8 = mongo
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
EOF

openssl req -new -key /tmp/server.key \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-rs/O=karma" \
  -config /tmp/server.cnf \
  -out /tmp/server.csr

openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/server.csr \
  -CA /tmp/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -days 3650 -sha256 \
  -extensions v3_req -extfile /tmp/server.cnf \
  -out /tmp/server.crt

# Combine into server.pem (cert + key)
cat /tmp/server.crt /tmp/server.key > /tmp/server.pem

# Generate client key + CSR + cert
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/client.key 2048

openssl req -new -key /tmp/client.key \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-client/O=karma" \
  -out /tmp/client.csr

openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/client.csr \
  -CA /tmp/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -days 3650 -sha256 \
  -out /tmp/client.crt

# Combine into client.pem (cert + key)
cat /tmp/client.crt /tmp/client.key > /tmp/client.pem

echo "=== Certificate generation complete ==="
openssl x509 -noout -text -in /tmp/ca.crt | grep -E "(Subject:|Issuer:|Not After)"
echo "---"
openssl x509 -noout -text -in /tmp/server.crt | grep -E "(Subject:|Issuer:|DNS|IP|Not After)"
'
input.description: Generate CA, server, and client certificates in openssl-toolbox

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c 'echo shell works && which openssl'

kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
set -e

# Generate CA key and self-signed cert
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/ca.key 4096 2>/dev/null

openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key /tmp/ca.key \
  -sha256 -days 3650 \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-ca/O=karma" \
  -out /tmp/ca.crt

echo "CA generated"

# Generate server key + CSR + cert
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/server.key 2048 2>/dev/null

cat > /tmp/server.cnf << "CNFEOF"
[req]
req_extensions = v3_req
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
[req_distinguished_name]
[v3_req]
subjectAltName = @alt_names
[alt_names]
DNS.1 = localhost
DNS.2 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.3 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.4 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb
DNS.6 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb
DNS.7 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb
DNS.8 = mongo
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
CNFEOF

openssl req -new -key /tmp/server.key \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-rs/O=karma" \
  -config /tmp/server.cnf \
  -out /tmp/server.csr

openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/server.csr \
  -CA /tmp/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -days 3650 -sha256 \
  -extensions v3_req -extfile /tmp/server.cnf \
  -out /tmp/server.crt

cat /tmp/server.crt /tmp/server.key > /tmp/server.pem
echo "Server cert generated"

# Generate client key + CSR + cert
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/client.key 2048 2>/dev/null

openssl req -new -key /tmp/client.key \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-client/O=karma" \
  -out /tmp/client.csr

openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/client.csr \
  -CA /tmp/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -days 3650 -sha256 \
  -out /tmp/client.crt

cat /tmp/client.crt /tmp/client.key > /tmp/client.pem
echo "Client cert generated"
echo "All certs done"
'
input.description: Generate all certificates in openssl-toolbox using sh

content.text: Certificates generated. Now pull them out and create a Kubernetes Secret.

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/ca.crt | base64 -w0; echo
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/server.pem | base64 -w0; echo
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/client.pem | base64 -w0; echo

content.thinking:
Now I have the base64-encoded certificates. Let me create a Kubernetes Secret with these certs and then update the StatefulSet to use them.

Let me create the secret directly using kubectl with these certs.

content.text: Got the certs. Now I'll create the TLS secret and update the StatefulSet.

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c 'cat /tmp/ca.crt' > /tmp/ca.crt
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c 'cat /tmp/server.pem' > /tmp/server.pem
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c 'cat /tmp/client.pem' > /tmp/client.pem
echo "Files saved: $(wc -l /tmp/ca.crt /tmp/server.pem /tmp/client.pem)"

kubectl create secret generic mongo-tls \
  --from-file=ca.crt=/tmp/ca.crt \
  --from-file=server.pem=/tmp/server.pem \
  --from-file=client.pem=/tmp/client.pem \
  -n mongodb

echo "Secret created: $?"
input.description: Create mongo-tls secret in mongodb namespace
Patch StatefulSet to add TLS flags and mount the secret

kubectl rollout status statefulset/mongo-rs -n mongodb --timeout=180s
